recent_sessions.title: Silk Road Myth Analysis — Regional Source Material
Story Arc Synthesis & Cosmological Deep Layer:
Silk Road Weapons & Orc Culture Expansion:

groups.title: Critical Decisions (Must resolve before anything below becomes canon)

items.text: Define the Mythic Ecosystem — Are Warrens/Holds/Hero Heaven/Threshold nodes in a living containment system? Is magic channeled through an infrastructure that can be damaged? (Proposed in synthesis; needs Mo approval)
Define the Sleeping Entity — Is there a cosmic force beneath/beyond the ecosystem that the ecosystem contains? What is its nature? (Proposed: geological/tectonic, non-conscious, not negotiable)
Decide the Entity's Nature — Pure force (no negotiation possible) vs. proto-conscious (can be communicated with)? (Recommendation: pure force — the horror is indifference)

Decide the Wizard's Awareness — Option A: tragic ignorance (doesn't know about entity), Option B: tragic hubris (knows, thinks he can manage it), Option C: cosmic manipulation (entity subtly influenced his 1,000-year obsession). Options are combinable (B+C recommended).

Decide: Do Heroes' Charms Strain the Ecosystem? — If yes, creates devastating tension (their greatest power is part of the problem). Recommendation: yes, but manageable — cost is real but benefit outweighs it.

Define the Liberation's Ecosystem Side Effects — Breaking binding spells was RIGHT but damaged ecosystem channels. Heroes should have repaired the damage ("cleaned up the rubble"). This is the incomplete charge. Must be framed with extreme care: liberation was unequivocally correct; the failure was not repairing side effects. (Extends the liberation_aftermath.md rewrite far beyond a timeline fix)

Define Threshold as Ecosystem Node — Threshold is a pressure regulator, not just a door. One of several, but the most critical. Breaching it with necromantic force = cracking containment. (Extends HERO_HEAVEN_THRESHOLD.md)

Define Three-Layer Revelation Structure — Surface quest (stop the Wizard) → ecosystem threat (his plan cracks containment) → deeper repair (heroes must heal what their incomplete charge left damaged). This is the overall story arc shape.

groups.title: Stakeholder Knowledge Distribution (Must resolve for mid-campaign planning)

items.text: Map High Elf awareness — They understand the ecosystem, withdrew because of cosmological fear, possess preventive (not restorative) knowledge. This answers the Elven cosmology question: not "celestial refugees" but "cosmologically literate beings who retreated from a fault line."
Map Dwarven archivist awareness — Chronicle of Ages contains empirical evidence of ecosystem degradation (disappearance patterns, magical anomaly data). They have data but need theory.

Map Orcish shamanic degradation — Ancestor communication becoming unreliable as ecosystem symptom. Ancient epic songs contain literal descriptions of the entity. Liberation side effects woven into their traditions.
Map human scholar suppression — Scholar's Purge (~1175 CE) was Wizard eliminating researchers. Surviving scholars are scattered, hunted, hold practical fragments.
Map Goblin/Halfling observational network — Trade disruptions, dead zones, charm failures = best continent-wide empirical dataset of ecosystem degradation. They don't know what their data means.
Design the information synthesis puzzle — Heroes as the only beings who can assemble distributed knowledge from all cultures. This is a core campaign mechanic, not just plot.
Phase 2: Wire up to sync flow (~35 min)

Test tag filtering, hash detection, batch limiting, force override Verify backward compatibility

Impact: Blocks GeoJSON automation, Python script creation Decisions Needed: Which fields: resources, factions, elevation, narrative_weight? Description building strategy (combine how?) Hybrid resources: FP (tactical, from Health) + Hope (strategic, from duality dice)
Conversion formulas: 1-3m → 2 FP OR 1 Hope (Tier 1); 4-6m → 3 FP OR 1 Hope + 1 Will (Tier 2); 7-10m → 2-3 Hope (Tier 3)
3. Awakened tools: 2d12 Hope (keep highest) — celestial dice
FP derived from Health stat, recovers with rest
Hope gained from duality dice + narrative choices + tier bonuses

Resist: Tool refuses certain actions (protective or restrictive?)
Hunger: Tool calls for use/restoration (genuine or manipulation?)
Know: Tool reveals fragmentary memories (truth or interpretation?)
Integration: Works with Charm system and duality dice
Fear Complication: Affects tool usage (specific mechanics TBD)
